reason: A total of 71/100 is a reasonable score given the well-documented issues with Hermione's film portrayal. The low arc (11/25) and motivations (11/25) align with widespread fan/critic consensus that the films stripped her of complexity, flaws, and character development, making her a near-flawless 'perfect' character with no real growth arc. The narrative_role score (14/25) is also defensible - the films gave her some of Ron's lines and agency, but removed key plot contributions (e.g. S.P.E.W., Rita Skeeter subplot). The personality score (19/25) feels slightly high given how thoroughly critics note her darker/crueler/more vulnerable book traits were omitted, but it's not egregiously wrong since her core traits (intelligence, loyalty, bravery) were preserved. The main caveat is that the justification fields are all empty, so there's nothing to fact-check there - the score itself is plausible but can't be fully validated without the reasoning.
